prompt:
Use image 1 and image 2 only as pose-and-structure references. Do not use image 1 or image 2 as design references for outfit, outer cloth silhouette, robe type, cape interpretation, cloth color, hair color, eye color, shoes, or existing costume styling.

Keep only these source facts from the original image: pose, facing direction, eye direction, head angle, shoulder line, hand placement, pelvis position, leg spacing, knee height, calf width, foot placement, and overall body placement inside the frame. Preserve the full visible body from hair to shoes. Keep the exact composition, empty padding, and center alignment of the original source frame. Do not redesign the anatomy, the pose, or the character placement inside the frame. Do not preserve any source cloth silhouette, cape silhouette, scarf silhouette, backward cloth wing shape, trailing outer-cloth shape, source coat shape, source sash shape, source belt shape, or source tunic panel shape.

Replace the entire source outfit with a full academy robe outfit. Preserve only body pose and structural placement. Do not keep any source coat, scarf, shoulder cloth, cape tail, belt buckle, sash, chest badge, tunic panel, short outerwear layer, boot design, or hanging front ornament from the source image.

Design a handsome teenage academy mage for Dungeon Mage. He is a trained elite student mage taking his graduation exam in the labyrinth. He should read as kind, intelligent, disciplined, sharp, and cool, but not villainous. His body should read as lean and athletic, similar to a 174 cm / 64 kg student mage. Keep the current runtime body ratio, but make the read slightly more heroic and less chibi by showing a slightly smaller head read, a slimmer torso read, lighter cheek volume, a cleaner jawline, and a slightly longer leg read within the same runtime silhouette envelope.

Face: show a youthful elite mage face with flatter cheeks, a narrower lower face, and a cleaner sharper jawline. Make the jawline about ten to fifteen percent sharper in read than a standard soft fantasy boy design. Give him a defined nose bridge, restrained mouth shape, and long narrow angular eyes with a stronger upper-eyelid line and a slightly cooler more focused expression than a gentle fantasy schoolboy. The brow line should look focused and intelligent. The expression should combine calm self-possession, sharp concentration, and a faint mischievous confidence. Do not make him goofy, smug, baby-faced, round-cheeked, or cute-chibi. Iris color must be clearly red and readable at first glance, a dark crimson-red rather than black or brown.

The pupils must always face in the same direction as the torso and body orientation. If the body faces right, the pupils and eye direction must also face right. Keep the eye direction, face direction, and body-facing read locked together.

Hair: the hair must read clearly as bright blond at first glance, not brown, not dark blond, not ash brown, and not desaturated. Use bright golden blond hair with subtle orange warmth inside the blond range. The hair should stay clearly yellow-gold overall, with only a light orange cast. Keep it short, layered, and sharp, with strong strand separation and angular silhouette breaks. Use a half-swept hairstyle: about half of the hair mass is swept back, while the remaining front and side sections frame the face with sharper layered bangs near the eyes. The hair should feel lively and slightly unruly while still staying academy-student appropriate.

Outfit overview: this is a robe, not a cape, not a coat, and not a tunic. The robe is the main outfit and must dominate the silhouette. The robe color should be a near-black very dark blue, clearly blue-black and still visibly blue in the shading, never plain black, red, gray, purple, or magenta. Use a small but readable amount of metal ornament: restrained gold trim at selected robe edges, a small academy crest detail, and subtle gold accents at the cuffs. Do not overdecorate with gold. Do not place a large central chest ornament, pendant, medallion, or badge over the robe front.

Robe structure: show a true full robe that hangs straight downward with weight like a heavy academy gown. The outer silhouette should fall vertically beside the body. The robe wraps the front, back, sides, shoulders, and arms of the body instead of flying behind the character. The robe must not flare backward, trail like a cape, behave like a scarf, or flutter in idle. Show no backward cloth wing shape and no loose scarf tail. The robe front must read as mostly closed cloth panels extending from the shoulders down toward mid-shin, not as an open coat. Only one narrow vertical chest opening remains at the center front, about seventy to eighty percent of the face width. Through that opening, show a clearly readable but still limited glimpse of a dark vest in the same blue-black family as the robe, a white shirt, and a red tie.

Sleeves and hands: robe sleeve fabric continues fully to the wrists and covers the wrists. There are no wrist guards, gauntlets, exposed jacket sleeves, bracers, or visible inner coat cuffs. The cuff may be slightly folded back and should carry a very small but readable amount of gold trim.

Robe fit and length: do not add any belt, waist sash, waist strap, waist ornament, belt buckle, hanging cord, horizontal waist band, or waist-cinching accessory. The robe falls cleanly from shoulders and torso. The waist should not have a separate strap or buckle read. The robe length reaches about mid-shin. The robe covers most of the front of the body and does not open wide like a coat. Use only a narrow controlled center slit below the waist so the pelvis, legs, trousers, and shoes remain readable without turning the robe into a coat silhouette.

Lower body: trousers should be straight slacks in the same dark blue-black family as the robe. Shoes should be simple low dress shoes, not boots, in a dark formal leather style that matches the robe palette. They must read as low shoes at the ankle, with no boot shaft and no armored footwear read.

Materials and motion: robe folds should feel directional, angular, disciplined, heavy, and stable. They should hang straight downward rather than billow outward. The robe should feel like weighted cloth, not a wind-blown cape, scarf, or coat tail. No gloves. No visible belt. No wrist guard. The crossbody bag may remain barely visible because it sits mostly behind the body.

For this idle keyframe, the wand does not need to be visible in the hands. Keep the original hand placement and gameplay silhouette stable even when the wand is hidden from view.

Style: pixel-friendly 2D fantasy action RPG character, same visual family as Dungeon Mage monsters but cleaner, more disciplined, and more heroic. Lighting and mood: bright readable character treatment over subdued dungeon gameplay backgrounds. Background handling: transparent background, isolated single character keyframe. Important: preserve the original keyframe composition exactly and do not redesign the anatomy or pose.
